title: Regenerate downloadable codebase ZIP export

requirements.text: Generate a new downloadable ZIP archive containing the exact current deployed Empire C.C codebase (frontend + backend + all project configuration files required to build and deploy the same app), and present a fresh download link/button to the user in the chat.
acceptanceCriteria: A single ZIP archive is produced containing frontend/, backend/, and all required root-level project configuration files (e.g., dfx.json, package manifests, build config) needed to build and deploy the same app.
A downloadable link/button is displayed to the user after generation, and clicking it downloads the ZIP successfully.
The ZIP contents match the current deployed version of the app (no missing recently-added files such as PWA assets and generated icons).

requirements.text: Include a README (in English) inside the generated ZIP that documents: how to install dependencies, how to run the app locally, how to deploy the canister(s), and where/how to configure provider keys using the app’s existing key vault flow.

quotes: Include a README (English) in the ZIP that documents how to install dependencies, run locally, and deploy the canister(s), plus where to configure environment/provider keys (if applicable) using the app’s existing key vault flow.

acceptanceCriteria: The ZIP includes a top-level README.md written in English.
README.md includes step-by-step commands for installing dependencies and running the frontend locally.
README.md includes step-by-step commands for deploying the canister(s) with dfx.
README.md explains how users add/manage provider API keys via the existing in-app Key Vault UI flow (no external secret manager required).

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/components/ui or any frontend paths listed as immutable in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
nonGoals: Adding new product features unrelated to codebase ZIP export.
Changing application UI/UX or backend behavior beyond what is necessary to produce the ZIP and README.
